title: Beach Route v2 and filing phase
summary: Now that the field season is ready to include beach, lane 4 should give the front half its first dedicated beach outing. This wave adds one evidence-backed beach Route v2 beat using the live lee-pocket, wrack, and dune spaces, then polishes filing and support language so the return feels as intentional as the forest pilots.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the first beach Route v2 outing handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Beach still lacks a dedicated gameplay-shaped outing even though the spaces and season framing now exist.

execution_order.goal: Implement the Shore Shelter beach Route v2 outing.
execution_order.why_now: A zone-ordered dune-to-wrack walk gives the starting biome a real notebook-first outing without adding another shell.

execution_order.goal: Review the Shore Shelter beach Route v2 outing.
execution_order.why_now: The first beach route should feel like an authored outing, not a hidden checklist.

execution_order.goal: Prepare notebook-ready notice priority and note-tabs beach-close polish.
execution_order.why_now: The beach return already works mechanically, but the final wrack inspect still lets nursery feedback replace the stronger route-ready cue.

execution_order.goal: Implement notebook-ready notice priority and one note-tabs beach close.
execution_order.why_now: Once the route exists, the front-half return should land through the existing notice and season-wrap seams instead of adding another shell.

execution_order.goal: Review the beach notebook-ready priority and note-tabs close.
execution_order.why_now: The front half should stay calm and legible, not grow another support shell.

guardrails: Reuse the existing Route v2 request, filing, and support seams whenever possible.
Do not add a new station page, notebook page, or route ledger.
The beach outing should use real observable evidence from the live beach spaces rather than generic inspect counts.
Beach support or filing polish should deepen existing note-tabs, place-tab, or route-marker behavior rather than add a new support row.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-4.md, docs/reports/2026-04-03-lane-4-front-half-route-v2-follow-ons.md, docs/reports/2026-04-03-beach-route-v2-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-04-03-beach-filing-support-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 4's front-half rebalance wave: the route itself now exists, so the next beach pass should stay narrowly inside the live notebook-return seams by making the route-ready cue outrank the overlapping nursery gather notice on the final wrack inspect and by giving note-tabs one short post-file beach close before Hidden Hollow takes over.
